PIPELINE_RULES.name: replace-szet-lc
PIPELINE_RULES.label: Replace ß → ss
PIPELINE_RULES.note: Swiss Standard German drops the eszett entirely — it is always written 'ss'. This rule enforces that for the lowercase form.

PIPELINE_RULES.name: replace-szet-uc
PIPELINE_RULES.label: Replace ẞ → SS
PIPELINE_RULES.note: Uppercase counterpart of replace-szet-lc — the capital eszett 'ẞ' becomes 'SS'.

PIPELINE_RULES.name: strip-non-keep-punct
PIPELINE_RULES.label: Strip non-keep punctuation
PIPELINE_RULES.note: Anything in string.punctuation NOT in ./-:,?! is removed, plus the German low quote „. The remaining ./-:,?! are kept on purpose so the later strip rules' digit-protected logic can run.

PIPELINE_RULES.name: normalize-digit-range
PIPELINE_RULES.label: Normalize digit-range hyphen → slash
PIPELINE_RULES.note: A spoken digit range like '10 - 12' is normalized to '10/12' — slash is the house convention for ranges.

PIPELINE_RULES.name: strip-and-lowercase-non-noun
PIPELINE_RULES.label: Strip + lowercase next non-noun
PIPELINE_RULES.note: Pass A: paired strip — match a terminator + capitalized word, strip the terminator and lowercase the next word IFF it is in the wordlist. German nouns are always capitalized, so the wordlist holds non-nouns (interrogatives, conjunctions, articles, etc.); matching is case-insensitive.

PIPELINE_RULES.name: strip-only-terminators-and-commas
PIPELINE_RULES.label: Strip-only terminators + commas
PIPELINE_RULES.note: Plain strip — catches lone .?! the paired pass missed, plus soft-pause commas. Digit-protected: '1,000' survives; 'Frau, Müller' loses the comma. Replacement is empty; no side effects.

PIPELINE_RULES.name: dictation-map
PIPELINE_RULES.label: Dictation map
PIPELINE_RULES.note: Spoken word → literal symbol. Word-bounded, case-insensitive. Multi-word phrases (e.g. 'eckige Klammer auf') MUST appear before their single-word components — main.py sorts the keys longest-first when building the alternation regex, so the longest phrase wins. Tradeoff: a dictation system cannot distinguish a noun 'Punkt' from the symbol 'Punkt' — substitutions are unconditional. Disable this rule for verbatim mode.

PIPELINE_RULES.name: space-before-closing-punct
PIPELINE_RULES.label: Remove space before closing punctuation
PIPELINE_RULES.note: Drop whitespace before closing punctuation so ' ,' / ' )' tighten up to ',' / ')'.

PIPELINE_RULES.name: space-after-opening-punct
PIPELINE_RULES.label: Remove space after opening punctuation
PIPELINE_RULES.note: Drop whitespace after an opening bracket so '( ' tightens to '('.

PIPELINE_RULES.name: collapse-multi-space
PIPELINE_RULES.label: Collapse multi-space
PIPELINE_RULES.note: Whisper segments are joined with an empty separator — if both adjacent segments end/start with whitespace the join produces an interior double-space that no other rule cleans (the punctuation-adjacent tidy rules do not match it).

PIPELINE_RULES.name: dedup-punct
PIPELINE_RULES.label: Dedup punctuation runs
PIPELINE_RULES.note: Whisper emits its own commas as soft pauses around dictation keywords, so after substitution we get runs like ',.' or ',;'. The user's intended mark wins: prefer any non-comma in the run, and within non-commas prefer the LAST one (the dictation came after the Whisper pause). A pure-comma run collapses to a single comma.

PIPELINE_RULES.name: tidy-newlines
PIPELINE_RULES.label: Tidy newlines
PIPELINE_RULES.note: Around a dictation-emitted newline there is often residue: a Whisper-emitted comma ('Müller, \n'), a trailing space, or punctuation that leaked through ('\n , Welt'). Collapse the neighborhood — optional whitespace + optional comma — on each side.

PIPELINE_RULES.name: capitalize-after-terminator
PIPELINE_RULES.label: Capitalize after sentence terminator
PIPELINE_RULES.note: After dictation inserts '.', '?', '!' or a newline, the following letter starts a new sentence/paragraph. Whisper does not know about our substitution, so it leaves that letter lowercase — fix it here.

PIPELINE_RULES.name: trim-edges
PIPELINE_RULES.label: Trim edges (always-last)
PIPELINE_RULES.note: Final, hardcoded terminal step — runs after the loop. lstrip + rstrip strip leading and trailing space/tab/CR but NOT '\n', so a user-dictated leading or trailing 'neue Zeile' at the edges of the utterance survives.
